body: Sketched the helper in a branch — going with a single theme_pssa() so we set fonts and palette once. Will open a PR after lab.
Confirmed the Kelvin tiles are the March pull. Adding a unit check that converts anything above 200 to Celsius before the overlay.
Leaflet version is up on the branch. Pan/zoom works but the legend still needs the colorblind-safe palette from #142.
Caching the geocoded points dropped the notebook 03 run from ~20 min to under 2. Pushed the cache file to data/ and gitignored the raw pulls.
Parameterized the year arg. Defaulting to 2022 5-year now; Prof. Marsh wants the older estimates kept for the comparison appendix.
Going with drop-and-document for zero-population tracts — imputing felt like it would muddy the displacement read. Noted it in the methods file.
Sorting descending by start date now. Fall mixer shows at the top again. Can you double-check on mobile before I merge?
RSVP button wired to the Eventbrite event id per card. Tested the fall mixer link end to end.
Fixed the list id — the Mailchimp audience id changed after the December merge. Form posts to the right list now.
Logger migration is in. Sentry breadcrumbs are way more readable now — the waypoint crash actually points at the render line.
Added exponential backoff plus a 5-minute request cache. 429s are gone in the last two runs. Thanks for the stats session in return.
Theme tokens documented with the contrast ratios. Picked an Okabe-Ito palette so it reads for deuteranopia too.
Build was failing because the footnote plugin wasn't registered. Added it to the config and the research-recap post renders now.
PostHog is in, cookieless mode, no banner needed. Already seeing which posts readers finish vs bounce.
Moved the scale_fill helpers here and re-pointed the thesis repo at them. One source of truth now.
Added the weighted bootstrap CI example with a small worked dataset. Will reuse it for the Alpaca back-test write-up.
Good catch — I'll move the legend into a bottom panel with guide_legend(nrow=2) so it stops colliding with the city labels at narrow widths. Will fix before the final presentation.
Confirmed it's the FIPS type mismatch — county codes come in as integers and drop the leading zeros. Casting both join keys to character with str_pad keeps all the tracts now.
Updated the README for the renv lockfile and the new data download script. Walked through it on a clean clone and the setup steps run end to end.
Guarded the render so an empty waypoints array short-circuits before the array access. The Sentry crash hasn't recurred in the last two deploys.
